summary: Fair‑quality audience tape of a solid Greek Theatre show, highlighted by Jack Straw > Bertha, To Lay Me Down and a memorable Never Trust A Woman sequence, though the mix is a bit tinny and Terrapin Station suffers from lyric slip‑ups.
review: The performance captures several standout moments – an excellent Jack Straw > Bertha opener, a top‑ten rendition of To Lay Me Down, and the newly introduced Never Trust A Woman that kept the energy high. Recording-wise the audience source is thin and slightly tinny with the post‑drums segment missing, yet it still conveys unique stage antics like the Hells Angels motorcycle during the drum solo.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical setlist and unique moments (show quality ~3.8/5) while noting a thin, tinny mix with missing post‑drums (recording quality ~3.2/5); combined weighted rating yields about 3.6.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Thin early‑80s audience mix that sounds a little tinny; post‑drums segment is missing; motorcycle noise from a Hells Angels bike is audible but not overpowering.

standout_songs: Let It Grow > Might As Well
show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely with a strong Jack Straw/Bertha opener and solid mid‑set tunes; the second set is unusually long with seven songs before drums, featuring the debut of Never Trust A Woman and a vibrant Cold Rain/Samson pairing. Unique stage elements (motorcycle, hot‑air balloons) add extra excitement.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Forgot lyrics during Terrapin Station and appeared embarrassed; Bob had to whisper the words to him.
band_member_comments.Bob: Had to step in and whisper Terrapin lyrics to Jerry, helping keep the song moving.
band_member_comments.Keys: Organ/keyboard work noted positively (review title “love that organ”).
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum solo captured the motorcycle revving on stage; post‑drums jam is missing but overall drum performance praised.
